note:
2016年，索菲·贾马尔的职业生涯出现了转折。这位骨骼研究人员和医生在加拿大被禁止终身接受联邦资金，此前一个委员会发现她犯有操纵数据、向调查人员提交伪造证据并将伪造数据归咎于一名研究助理的罪行。她还被勒令偿还从加拿大卫生研究所获得的超过25.3万加元的资金。然后，在2018年3月，她被吊销了医生执照。
两年后，贾马尔又回到了医学界。据多伦多太阳报报道，在她向安大略省医师和外科学院提交证据表明她的不当行为是她长期精神健康问题的直接结果后，她的行医执照被恢复。她现在可以回到内分泌科执业，只要她不进行临床研究，继续治疗她的精神健康问题。
贾马尔是否适合回去治疗病人，这是加拿大的医疗机构和机构决定的事情。事实是，犯有研究不端行为的科学家通常只需一记耳光就能逃脱惩罚--也就是说，如果他们一开始就被抓住的话。今年早些时候公布的一项针对8所欧洲大学1100多名研究人员的调查发现，大量研究不当行为没有上报，职业生涯早期的研究人员揭发的可能性最小。
note_en:
I n 2016, Sophie Jamal’s career took a turn for the worse. The bone researcher and physician was  banned from federal funding for life in Canada after a committee found her guilty of manipulating data, presenting fabricated evidence to investigators, and blaming a research assistant for the fudged data. She was also ordered to pay back more than 253,000 Canadian dollars she had received in funding from the Canada Institute of Health Research. Then, in March 2018, she was stripped of her medical license.
 Two years later, Jamal is back on the medical scene. According to The Toronto Sun, her medical license  was reinstated after she presented evidence to the College of Physicians and Surgeons of Ontario showing that her misconduct was a direct result of her long-term mental health problems. She can now return to practicing as an endocrinologist, as long as she doesn’t conduct clinical research and continues therapy for her mental health problems.
 Whether it’s fit for Jamal to return to treating patients is a matter for medical bodies and institutions in Canada to decide. What’s true is that scientists guilty of research misconduct often get away with nothing more than a slap on the wrist — that is, if they’re even caught in the first place.  A survey of more than 1,100 researchers at eight European universities published earlier this year found that a large amount of research misconduct goes unreported, with early-career researchers the least likely to blow the whistle.
title: 是时候严肃对待研究欺诈了
title_en: It's time to get serious about research fraud

note: 芝加哥(WLS)--被指控侵入伊利诺伊州选举数据库的12名俄罗斯人可能曾担任情报人员，但i-team已经了解到，他们并没有利用高于平均水平的计算机智能来实施这起抢劫案。根据联邦调查人员的说法，俄罗斯黑客使用了信息窃贼使用的一种常见的计算机技巧。这起电子入侵事件发生在2016年7月的伊利诺伊州选举委员会，是通过一种名为SQL注入的黑客技术进行的。根据上周提交的针对12名俄罗斯特工的联邦起诉书和州选举官员的信息，据信约50万伊利诺伊州选民的姓名、地址、出生日期、驾照号码和部分社保号码被盗。伊利诺伊州选举委员会发言人马特·迪特里希(Matt Dietrich)表示：对于很多选民来说，他们非常担心在我投票后会有俄罗斯黑客入侵并窃取我的选票。伊利诺伊州没有一张选票因为两年前的情况而改变。没有一张选票试图以此为基础进行更改。这不是目标。如果目标仅仅是获取访问、窃取信息和挑拨离间，那么俄罗斯的行动似乎成功了。伊利诺伊州民主党参议员迪克·德宾(Dick Durbin)周二表示，俄罗斯人侵入了选举官员和供应商的计算机网络，以窃取选民数据和其他信息。我们知道俄罗斯干预了2016年大选，我们也知道我们应该为俄罗斯干预2018年中期选举做好准备。SQL是结构化查询语言(Structural Query Language)的首字母缩写，是一种数据库编程语言。SQL注入是一种常见的网络欺骗手段，用于非法访问政府、金融、商业和私人计算机。专家估计，每10个数据泄露中就有8个是由SQL注入造成的。黑客青睐的策略通常以在公共网页表单上键入某些命令开始，以广泛访问网站的服务器结束。在伊利诺伊州的案例中，黑客在选举数据库搜索框中输入了一个特制的代码后，记录被盗，委员会不得不关闭登记10天。当时，联邦调查局向全美各州选举当局发出了特别警告。数字指纹多次引导调查人员远离真正的罪犯。正如i-Team在2016年透露的那样，伊利诺伊州的网络攻击者似乎与土耳其一个政党有关联。然而，两年后的今天，联邦特工表示，他们已经追踪到这次袭击发生在俄罗斯。伊利诺伊州选举官员表示，他们已经采取措施保护选民数据库和网络应用程序的安全。州调查人员确定，处理器使用率已飙升至100%，但没有任何解释。他们说，对服务器日志的分析表明，沉重的负载是在无纸在线选民申请(POVA)网站的申请状态页面上快速重复数据库查询的结果。州政府官员表示，已经制定了新的协议，以防止再次发生此类恶意攻击。我们从对攻击的分析中得出结论，我们可以合理地预计各种IVRS(伊利诺伊州选民登记系统)密码被攻破。这些密码包括选举当局、其工作人员、内部SBE用户、供应商和Web服务的密码。为了确保攻击者不能用被盗的密码访问系统的任何部分。&#34；根据内部报告，当局重置所有IVRS密码，以迫使用户在下次登录时更改其密码；引入增强的密码复杂性要求(长度、特殊字符、数字等)；强制所有IVRS用户进行双因素令牌登录；向IVRS数据库添加密码加密，以便即使是SBE员工也无法再破译存储的密码。迪特里希说：两年前入侵事件发生后，我们安装了新的防火墙硬件和软件，我们专门派人负责网络安全，以支持系统，确保这种情况不会再次发生。SQL注入策略已被世界各地的黑客用来进入公司数据库、银行和政府机构。击键技巧通常利用允许未经授权访问的数据库中常见的漏洞。编者按：这个故事的早期版本对SQL的定义不准确。自那以后，这一点已经得到纠正。
note_en: CHICAGO (WLS) -- The twelve Russians charged with hacking into Illinois' election database may have worked as intelligence agents, but the I-Team has learned it didn't take above-average computer intelligence to pull off the heist.  Russian hackers used a common computer trick employed by info-thieves, according to federal investigators.   The electronic intrusion, which occurred in July of 2016 at the Illinois State Board of Election, was via a hacking technique called "SQL injection." The names, addresses, dates of birth, driver's license numbers and partial Social Security numbers of about 500,000 Illinois voters are believed to have been stolen, according to federal indictments handed up last week against a dozen Russian operatives and information from state election officials.  "For a lot of voters there has been a lot of fear that there is going to be Russian hacking and stealing my vote after I cast my vote" said Matt Dietrich, spokesperson for the Illinois State Board of Elections. "Not one vote was changed in Illinois based on what happened 2 years ago. Not one vote was attempted to be changed based on that. That was not the object."  If the object was to simply gain access, grab information and sow discord, then the Russian operation seems to have succeeded.  "The Russians hacked into the computer networks of election officials and vendors in order to steal voter data and other information" said Sen. Dick Durbin , D-Illinois on Tuesday. "We know that Russia meddled in the 2016 election and we know that we should be gearing up for the Russians to interfere with the 2018 midterm election as well."  SQL, an acronym for Structured Query Language, is a database programming language. An "SQL injection" is a common piece of cyber-trickery used to illegally gain access to government, financial, business and private computers. Experts estimate that 8 of every 10 data breaches occur as a result of SQL injection.   The favored tactic of hackers usually begins with certain commands typed on a public web form and ends with broad access to the site's server. In the case of Illinois, after hackers typed a specially-crafted code into the election database search box, records were stolen and the board had to shut down registration for ten days.  At the time the FBI put out a special alert to state election authorities across the U.S. Many times the digital fingerprint leads investigators away from the actual culprits. As the I-Team revealed in 2016, Illinois cyber-attackers appeared to be a connected to a Turkish political party. Now though, two years later, federal agents say they have traced the attack to Russia.  Illinois election officials say they have taken measures to secure voter databases and web applications.  "Processor usage had spiked to 100% with no explanation"  state investigators determined. "Analysis of server logs revealed that the heavy load was a result of rapidly repeated database queries on the application status page of the Paperless Online Voter Application (POVA) web site" they said.  State officials say new protocols are in place to prevent another such malicious attack.   "We concluded from the analysis of the attack that we could reasonably expect that various IVRS (Illinois Voter Registration System) passwords were compromised. These passwords included those of election authorities, their staffs, internal SBE users, vendors, and web services. In order to ensure that attackers could not access any portion of the system with stolen passwords."  According to internal reports, authorities reset all IVRS passwords in order to force users to change their password at next login; introduced enhanced password complexity requirements (length, special characters, numbers, etc.); mandated two-factor token login for all IVRS users; added password encryption to the IVRS database so that stored passwords could no longer be deciphered, even by SBE employees.  "In the wake of the breach 2 years ago we installed new firewall hardware and software we devoted personnel specifically to cyber security to shore up the system to make sure it wasn't going to happen again" said Dietrich.  The SQL injection ploy has been used by hackers around the world to gain access to corporate databases, banks and government agencies. The keystroke trick usually exploits vulnerabilities that are commonly present in databases that allow unauthorized access.   EDITOR'S NOTE: An earlier version of this story had an inaccurate definition of SQL. This has since been corrected.
title: 俄罗斯人是如何渗透伊利诺伊州选举计算机的
title_en: How the Russians penetrated Illinois election computers

note:
苹果公司因涉嫌拒绝帮助那些遭受iTunes礼品卡诈骗的人而被起诉。该公司已被提起11项集体诉讼。
苹果称无法追踪或退还…卡的价值，因此被指控撒谎。
有大量的骗局，涉及预付礼品卡，如商店卡和iTunes礼品卡。以下是联邦贸易委员会对它们的描述。
许多不同种类的冒名顶替者要求你用礼品卡付款。有人可能会打电话给你，声称自己是美国国税局的人，正在追回税款或罚款。打电话的人可能会说他是技术支持的，想要钱来修理你的电脑。打电话的人甚至可能会说她是有紧急情况的家庭成员，现在需要钱。
但他们都有一个共同点，那就是迫切需要你立即汇款[…]。一旦你买了卡，呼叫者就会要求你在卡的背面输入礼品卡的卡号和密码。这些数字可以让他们立即拿到你存到卡上的钱。一旦他们这么做了，骗子和你的钱就不见了，通常没有任何踪迹。
诈骗者通常用商店卡购买笔记本电脑和智能手机等高价值产品，然后再出售。ITunes礼品卡诈骗的工作方式通常略有不同，通常被用来购买骗子拥有的付费应用程序，因此当苹果支付时，他们会获得70%的钱。
诉讼称，苹果告诉诈骗受害者，一旦钱花完就无能为力了，但辩称这不是真的。事实上，苹果持有100%的资金，期限为4-6周，从购买的应用程序到苹果向开发者支付的费用。在此期间，该公司可以退还卡价值的100%。
此外，苹果还收取30%的佣金，因此即使在诈骗者拿到钱之后，也总是有能力退还这么多钱。
绝大多数受害者没有向联邦贸易委员会(“FTC”)报告诈骗。然而，即使是2015-2019年期间向联邦贸易委员会报告的有限的iTunes礼品卡骗局也超过了9350万美元，金额每年都在大幅增加。这些公开报告的美元金额只包括填写详细的在线FTC表格要求提供个人信息的受害者子集的损失。因此，这个9,350万美元的数字似乎只是冰山一角。
即使只有10%的诈骗受害者向联邦贸易委员会报案(与当地警方、总检察长办公室、苹果公司或任何人相比)，iTunes礼品卡诈骗金额也将接近10亿美元，苹果公司将保留3亿美元的佣金。
许多诈骗受害者是老年人，因此11项指控中有3项指控苹果违反了旨在保护老年人免受财务滥用的法律。

note_en:
Apple is being sued for allegedly refusing to help those who have fallen victim to a  iTunes gift card scam. An 11-count class action lawsuit has been filed against the company.
 Apple is accused of lying when it says that there is no way to trace or refund the value of the cards …
  There are a large number of scams which involve pre-paid gift cards like store cards and  iTunes gift cards. Here’s how the  FTC describes them.
 Many different kinds of imposters ask you to pay with gift cards. Someone might call you and claim to be from the IRS, collecting back taxes or fines. The caller might say he’s from tech support, asking for money to fix your computer. The caller might even say she’s a family member with an emergency and needs money right now.
 But they all have in common an urgent need for you to send money right away […] Once you buy the card, the caller then will demand the gift card number and PIN on the back of the card. Those numbers let them immediately get the money you loaded onto the card. And once they’ve done that, the scammers and your money are gone, usually without a trace.
 With store cards, the scammers usually buy high-value products like laptops and smartphones, which are then sold. iTunes gift card scams usually work in a slightly different way, typically being used to buy paid apps owned by the scammers, so they receive 70% of the money when paid by Apple.
 The  lawsuit says that Apple tells scam victims there is nothing that can be done once the money has been spent, but argues that this isn’t true. In fact, Apple holds 100% of the funds for a period of 4-6 weeks, between the apps being purchased and Apple paying the developer. During this time, the company is in a position to refund 100% of the card value.
 Additionally, Apple takes a 30% commission, so would  always be in a position to refund this much, even after the scammer has been paid.
  The overwhelming majority of victims do not report the scam to the Federal Trade Commission (“FTC”). Yet even the limited iTunes gift card scams reported to the FTC during 2015-2019 exceed $93.5 million, with the dollar amounts increasing significantly each year. These publicly reported dollar amounts include the losses of only the subset of victims who fill out a detailed online FTC form asking for their personal information. As a result, this $93.5 million figure appears to be only the tip of the iceberg.
 Even if only 10% of scam victims report to the FTC (versus local police, attorney general offices, Apple, or nobody at all), the iTunes gift card scam would approach $1 billion, with Apple retaining $300 million in commissions.
 Many scam victims are elders, so three of the eleven counts accuse Apple of being in violation of laws designed to protect elderly people from financial abuse.

note:
有一段时间，TikTok似乎可以避开技术冲击。毕竟，一款短小的视频应用程序有什么问题呢？它展示了一群青少年和20多岁的年轻人跳着精心编排的舞蹈，滑旱冰，在有影响力的豪宅里闲逛，切入逼真的蛋糕。
在过去的一年里，随着TikTok成为全球最受欢迎的应用之一，它积累了许多与其他大型社交网络相同的问题。除了所有无害的Z世代乐趣，还有TikTok阴谋论、TikTok错误信息和TikTok极端主义。甚至还有一些活动人士使用TikTok影响我们的选举，其中包括一个由青少年和韩国流行歌迷组成的网络，他们声称自己上个月使用TikTok破坏了特朗普总统在俄克拉何马州塔尔萨举行的集会，用虚假身份注册了门票。
除了一个事实，所有这些都可能被忽视或原谅。TikTok隶属于中国最大的科技公司之一ByteDance。
最近几周，TikTok的中国所有权已成为立法者、监管机构和隐私权活动人士密切关注的话题。特朗普正在考虑采取措施，在美国禁止这款应用。富国银行(Wells Fargo)等公司以及美国运输安全管理局(Transportation Security Administration)等政府机构已指示员工从工作手机上删除TikTok，因为担心TikTok可能被用于监控或间谍活动。
为了应对越来越大的压力，TikTok将自己包裹在美国国旗下。该公司在华盛顿雇佣了一小批说客，聘请了一位美国首席执行官(前迪士尼高管凯文·梅耶尔(Kevin Mayer))，据报道，该公司正在研究将公司的多数股权出售给美国投资者。
TikTok发言人杰米·法瓦扎(Jamie Favazza)在一份声明中表示，除了首席执行官，这家社交网络还有一名美国人担任首席信息安全官，另一名美国人担任安全主管。
“自2020年初以来，我们在美国的员工数量增加了两倍，”她说，“我们计划在未来三年内在德克萨斯州、纽约和佛罗里达州等地再招聘1万人。”
有理由担心一家中资公司会吸引数百万美国人的注意力和数据-特别是像ByteDance这样的公司，它有向中国统治政权屈膝的历史。与所有中国科技公司一样，ByteDance被要求遵守中国的审查法律，根据中国的国家安全法，它可能会被迫向中国政府提供用户数据。议员们还担心TikTok可能会被用来向美国年轻人宣传亲华宣传，或者审查政治敏感内容。
法瓦扎说，TikTok将美国用户数据存储在弗吉尼亚州和新加坡。她补充说，该公司的内容审核工作由美国团队领导，不受任何外国政府的影响，TikTok没有也不会向中国政府提供数据。
也有理由怀疑TikTok最大批评者的动机。包括特朗普在内的许多保守派政客似乎更关心对中国表现强硬，而不是防止TikTok用户受到潜在伤害。像Facebook这样的硅谷科技公司，其高管已经就中国科技收购的危险发出了警告，它们肯定希望看到监管机构击败他们的主要竞争对手之一。
老实说：我不相信TikTok对美国国家安全构成紧急威胁的说法。或者，更准确地说，我不相信TikTok天生就比其他任何从美国人那里收集数据的中国人拥有的应用程序对美国人更具威胁性。如果说TikTok是一种威胁，那么微信、阿里巴巴和流行视频游戏英雄联盟也是一种威胁，该游戏的开发商Riot Games隶属于中国的腾讯。
而且，如果不竖起我们自己版本的中国长城，禁止每一家中资科技公司在美国运营是不可能的-这一激进的举措会引发人们对审查和威权控制的担忧-我们需要找到一种方法，让中国的应用程序和美国的民主共存。
这里有一个想法：与其禁止TikTok，或者强迫ByteDance向美国人出售它，为什么不以身作则，把它变成现有的最透明、最保护隐私、最受道德治理的技术平台？
作为一款外资应用，TikTok在某些方面比美国科技平台更容易监管。(据报道，监管它的一种方式是，美国外国投资委员会(Committee On Foreign Investment In The United States)对ByteDance 2017年收购TikTok的前身应用Musical.ly的国家安全审查已经在进行中。)。美国政府还有很多事情可以做，以确保TikTok在我们的信息生态系统中发挥负责任的作用，而不是完全摆脱它。它可能要求该公司将其软件的关键部分开源，包括决定用户显示哪些帖子的机器学习算法。它可能会向TikTok施压，要求其接受对其数据收集做法的定期审计，并开放其内部内容审核指南，征求公众意见。正如关于美中关系的博客“互联”(InterConnected)的作者凯文·徐(Kevin Xu)指出的那样，字节跳动可以实施严格的内部控制，以防止其中国员工访问TikTok的任何系统，并将这些控制开源，以便外部人士可以核实这种分离。
中间派智库新美国(New America)的网络政策研究员萨姆·萨克斯(Samm Sacks)告诉我，为TikTok提出的一些解决方案-比如向美国投资者出售自己-并不能解决核心问题。例如，美国所有的TikTok仍然可以合法地将数据出售给第三方数据经纪人，然后第三方数据经纪人可以将数据反馈给中国当局。
萨克斯说，相反，美国政府应该制定一部强有力的联邦隐私法，既能保护TikTok用户的数据，又不会完全禁止该应用程序。
“让我们来解决手头的问题，”她说。“如果担心的是数据安全，保护数据的最佳方式是将TikTok置于显微镜下，并就他们如何使用和保留数据制定真正强大和可执行的规则。”
迫使TikTok以一种完全透明的方式运营，将对缓解美国人的恐惧大有裨益。它可能会成为一种新的科技监管模式的测试案例，这种模式不仅可以改善中国所有的科技公司的问责和责任，也可以改善美国公司的责任。
本质上，TikTok的很多恐惧因素都可以归结为缺乏信息。今年3月，TikTok宣布将开设“透明中心”，独立审计师可以在那里审查其内容审核做法。该公司还开始发布“透明度报告”，类似于Facebook和Twitter发布的报告，概述了它从世界各地政府收到的各种删除请求。
但我们仍然不知道TikTok的算法是如何编程的，也不知道为什么他们会把哪些视频显示给哪些用户。我们不知道它是如何使用它正在收集的数据的，也不知道它是如何制定和执行它的规则的。我们应该知道这些事情-不仅是关于TikTok，也是关于美国的社交媒体应用程序。
毕竟，Facebook、Instagram、YouTube、Twitter和Snapchat在数百万美国人的生活中扮演着巨大的角色，多年来，它们的运营保密程度是很少有其他像它们这样重要的公司被允许的。我们对这些平台的内部运作知之甚少，往往是在事件发生多年后才知道的，是从内幕泄密或悔改的前员工那里收集的。
“我认为TikTok有点转移视线，”Facebook前首席安全官、斯坦福大学(Stanford University)教授亚历克斯·斯塔莫斯(Alex Stamos)在接受采访时告诉我。斯塔莫斯说，归根结底，与总体上应该如何对待跨国科技巨头的问题相比，如何处理TikTok的问题是次要的。
他说：“这是一个机会，可以拿出一个深思熟虑的模式，如何监管在美国和中国运营的公司，无论它们的所有权如何。”
换句话说，关于TikTok命运的辩论真的应该是一场辩论，讨论所有那些娱乐、告知和影响数十亿人的大型科技公司应该如何运营，以及应该对它们提出什么要求，无论它们的总部设在中国、哥本哈根还是加州。
如果我们能弄清楚如何处理TikTok-一款拥有真正创意文化的应用程序，以及数百万热爱它的美国年轻人-我们所做的将不仅仅是保留一个世界级的浪费时间的产品。在放任大型科技平台横行多年之后，我们会想出一个让它们处于控制之下的模式。
note_en:
For a while, it seemed that TikTok might dodge the techlash. After all, what could be problematic about a short-form video app featuring a bunch of teenagers and 20-somethings doing  choreographed dances,  roller skating, hanging out in  influencer mansions and cutting into  photorealistic cakes?
  In the past year, as it has become one of the most popular apps in the world, TikTok has accumulated many of the same problems that other large-scale social networks have. In addition to all the harmless Gen Z fun, there are  TikTok conspiracy theories,  TikTok misinformation and  TikTok extremism. There are even activists using TikTok to influence our elections, including a network of teenagers and K-pop fans who  claimed they used the app to sabotage President Trump’s rally in Tulsa, Okla., last month by registering for tickets under false identities.
 All of this might have been overlooked or forgiven, except for one fact. TikTok is owned by ByteDance, one of the largest tech companies in China.
 TikTok’s Chinese ownership has become a subject of intense scrutiny by lawmakers, regulators and privacy activists in recent weeks. Mr. Trump is  considering taking steps to ban the app in the United States. Companies including Wells Fargo, and government agencies including the Transportation Security Administration, have  instructed their employees to delete TikTok from their work phones because of concerns that it could be used for surveillance or espionage.
 In response to the mounting pressures, TikTok is wrapping itself in the American flag. The company has  hired a small army of lobbyists in Washington, has  brought in an American chief executive (the former Disney executive Kevin Mayer) and  is reportedly exploring selling a majority stake in the company to American investors.
 Jamie Favazza, a TikTok spokeswoman, said in a statement that in addition to the chief executive, the social network had an American as its chief information security officer and another as its head of safety.
 “We’ve tripled the number of employees in the U.S. since the start of 2020,” she said, “with plans to hire 10,000 more people over the next three years in places like Texas, New York and Florida.”
 There are legitimate concerns about a Chinese-owned company capturing the attention and data of millions of Americans — especially one like ByteDance, which  has a history of bending the knee to the country’s ruling regime. Like all Chinese tech companies, ByteDance is required to abide by Chinese censorship laws, and it could be forced to give user data to the Chinese government under the country’s national security law. Lawmakers have also raised concerns that TikTok could be used to promote pro-China propaganda to young Americans, or censor politically sensitive content.
 Ms. Favazza said TikTok stored American user data in Virginia and Singapore. She added that the company’s content moderation efforts were led by U.S.-based teams and not influenced by any foreign government, and that TikTok had not and would not give data to the Chinese government.
 There are also reasons to be skeptical of the motives of TikTok’s biggest critics. Many conservative politicians, including Mr. Trump, appear to care more about appearing tough on China than preventing potential harm to TikTok users. And Silicon Valley tech companies like Facebook, whose executives  have warned of the dangers of a Chinese tech takeover, would surely like to see regulators kneecap one of their major competitors.
 I’ll be honest: I don’t buy the argument that TikTok is an urgent threat to America’s national security. Or, to put it more precisely, I am not convinced that TikTok is inherently more threatening to Americans than any other Chinese-owned app that collects data from Americans. If TikTok is a threat, so are WeChat, Alibaba and League of Legends, the popular video game, whose maker, Riot Games, is owned by China’s Tencent.
 And since banning every Chinese-owned tech company from operating in America wouldn’t be possible without erecting our own version of China’s Great Wall — a drastic step that would raise concerns about censorship and authoritarian control — we need to figure out a way for Chinese apps and American democracy to coexist.
  Here’s an idea: Instead of banning TikTok, or forcing ByteDance to sell it to Americans, why not make an example of it by turning it into the most transparent, privacy-protecting, ethically governed tech platform in existence?
 As a foreign-owned app, TikTok is, in some ways, easier to regulate than an American tech platform would be. (One way of regulating it, a national security review by the Committee on Foreign Investment in the United States of ByteDance’s 2017 acquisition of Musical.ly, TikTok’s predecessor app, is  already reportedly underway.) And there is plenty more the U.S. government could do to ensure that TikTok plays a responsible role in our information ecosystem without getting rid of it altogether. It could require the company to open-source key parts of its software, including the machine-learning algorithms that determine which posts users are shown. It could pressure TikTok to submit to regular audits of its data-collection practices, and open up its internal content moderation guidelines for public comment. As Kevin Xu, the author of Interconnected, a blog about United States-China relations, points out, ByteDance could  impose strict internal controls to prevent its Chinese employees from accessing any of TikTok’s systems, and open-source those controls so that outsiders could verify the separation.
 Samm Sacks, a cyberpolicy fellow at the centrist think tank New America, told me that some of the solutions being proposed for TikTok — such as selling itself to American investors — wouldn’t address the core problems. An American-owned TikTok could still legally sell data to third-party data brokers, for example, which could then feed it back to the Chinese authorities.
 Instead, Ms. Sacks said, the American government should enact a strong federal privacy law that could protect TikTok users’ data without banning the app altogether.
 “Let’s solve for the problems at hand,” she said. “If the concern is data security, the best way to secure the data is to put TikTok under the microscope, and put in place really robust and enforceable rules about how they’re using and retaining data.”
 Forcing TikTok to operate in a radically transparent way would go a long way toward assuaging Americans’ fears. And it could become a test case for a new model of tech regulation that could improve the accountability and responsibility of not just Chinese-owned tech companies but American ones, too.
 At its core, a lot of the TikTok fear factor comes down to a lack of information. In March, TikTok  announced that it would open “transparency centers” where independent auditors could examine its content moderation practices. The company has also begun  releasing “transparency reports,” similar to those issued by Facebook and Twitter, outlining the various takedown requests it gets from governments around the world.
 But we still don’t know how TikTok’s algorithms are programmed, or why they’re showing which videos to which users. We don’t know how it’s using the data it’s collecting, or how it makes and enforces its rules. We should know these things — not just about TikTok, but about American social media apps, too.
 After all, Facebook, Instagram, YouTube, Twitter and Snapchat are playing a huge role in the lives of millions of Americans, and for years, they have operated with a degree of secrecy that few other companies of their importance have been allowed. What little we understand about these platforms’ inner workings is often learned years after the fact, gleaned from insider leaks or repentant former employees.
  “I think TikTok is a bit of a red herring,” Alex Stamos, Facebook’s former chief security officer and a professor at Stanford University, told me in an interview. Ultimately, Mr. Stamos said, the question of what to do about TikTok is secondary to the question of how multinational tech giants in general should be treated.
 “This is a chance to come up with a thoughtful model of how to regulate companies that operate in both the U.S. and China, no matter their ownership,” he said.
 The debate over TikTok’s fate, in other words, should really be a debate about how all of the big tech companies that entertain, inform and influence billions of people should operate, and what should be required of them, whether they’re based in China or Copenhagen or California.
 If we can figure out how to handle TikTok — an app with a  genuinely creative culture, and millions of American young people who love it — we’ll have done a lot more than preserving a world-class time-waster. We’ll have figured out a model for getting big tech platforms under control, after years of letting them run amok.
title: 纽约时报

note:
多年来，我一直在重读最喜欢的科幻短篇小说，同时也在寻找有没有什么东西是我想不到的。考虑到我读的书不像以前那么多，我也一直在努力追赶最近的作家。(总体而言，科幻短篇小说市场在某种程度上正在下滑，但最近仍然有一些令人难以置信的故事。)。
每个作者一个故事。当然，在这份名单上有很多人，我可以毫不费力地一口气说出多个应得的条目。这条规则的一个副作用是，它给名单带来了相当多的有机多样性，否则名单上会有更多受欢迎的作家。
没有什么是小说长度，但除了短篇小说，你会发现很多中篇小说和中篇小说。
我也不包括那些我喜欢小说，但没有任何短篇小说的作家，我知道这让我惊叹不已。
这里的一切都可以合理地归类为科幻小说，尽管有些人比其他人更接近传统的科幻小说，至少与恐怖、奇幻和哲学小说相近。
我使用了各种清单和推荐来唤醒我的记忆，并搜索出我可能不熟悉的作品。但我已经阅读了这里的所有内容，我不认为我会仅仅因为它的预期而收录任何东西。这些都是我个人最喜欢的，或者至少是某位作家最喜欢的故事中的一枚硬币。我的目标确实是在不同的时间段、风格和题材上做出一些全面的选择，但我认为这里的任何东西都可以被现代读者喜欢，而不需要太多的考虑。
这些故事中有许多可以在网上找到，也有一些可以在流行的精品集中找到。
无论如何，我的初始列表(按时间顺序排序)如下。虽然这是每个作者一个故事，但我确实在评论中包含了指向其他作品的指针。我一直小心翼翼地避免剧透，但如果你想要完全冷淡地进入故事，无论如何，就从这份书名和作者名单开始吧。
这份榜单上最早的故事来自多产的维多利亚时代作家赫伯特·乔治·威尔斯(Herbert George Wells)。威尔斯以各种体裁写作，包括社会评论和历史。然而，他最令人难忘的是他的科幻小说，如“世界大战”(众所周知，这部小说被改编成广播剧和质量参差不齐的电影)。故事使用了虚构的手段，一名男子讲述了他对未来时光的梦想，在梦中，他是一名政治人物，已经退出生活，与卡普里岛上的一名年轻女子生活在一起，尽管这种退出使对手能够与未来主义飞机展开一场大战。威尔斯后来在他的小说“即将到来的事物的形状”的概念上建立了这个概念，这本身就是1936年同名电影的松散基础。
鲁迪亚德·吉卜林在这个故事发表几年后获得了小说文学奖。与威尔斯不同的是，吉卜林不是因为科幻小说而被人们记住；相反，他主要与故事和诗歌联系在一起，这些故事和诗歌通常以殖民时期的印度为背景，他早年的大部分生活都是在那里出生和生活的。“夜邮报”是吉卜林仅有的两个科幻故事之一。两者都涉及空中管制委员会(AIR Board Of Control)的工作和活动，这是一个21世纪虚构的组织，负责管理全世界的飞艇和其他空中交通，并充当事实上的世界政府。
除了这三位维多利亚时代的英国作家外，福斯特的名声还部分来自一部小说“印度之行”，这部小说关注的是英国统治后期通过印度的视角看待的东西方关系，以及其他主要关注英国阶级差异的小说。然而，福斯特写道，“机器停止”是对H·G·威尔斯(H.G.Wells)早期天堂之一的反作用。[“时间机器的伊洛伊”(The Eloi Of The Time Machine)。]。在这部中篇小说中，人类生活在地下，依靠一台巨大的机器来满足自己的需求。有一些类似于视频会议和互联网的技术。但是机器开始出故障了，没有人记得如何修理它。这个故事被广泛认为是早期科幻小说中最有先见之明的短篇小说之一。
惠普·洛夫克拉夫特的主要名声是恐怖小说作家。然而，他的故事是克苏鲁神话的一部分(例如这个)，借用了通常与科幻小说相关的前提，如外星人入侵、其他维度，以及对人类文化和生理进化的干扰。例如，这部中篇小说普及了古代宇航员的理论，因为它详细描述了一次灾难性的南极大陆探险事件，以及由叙述者密斯卡通大学(Miskatonic University)的威廉·戴尔(William Dyer)博士率领的一群探险家在那里发现了什么。
这个故事最初发表在“令人震惊的科幻”杂志上，是科幻小说中使用口袋宇宙概念的早期例子。生物化学家开发了一种合成生命形式，它的生命(和发明)速度远远快于人类。这位名叫基德的生物化学家是他们的上帝，他对他们施展他的力量，并从他们身上获利。鲟鱼一般不是很出名，但这个特殊的故事经常出现在收藏品和最佳名单中。
这篇短篇小说最初是由阿根廷作家兼图书管理员豪尔赫·路易斯·博尔赫斯(Jorge Luis Borges)用西班牙语撰写的，与其说是通常意义上的科幻故事，不如说是一次哲学探索。它要求你想象一个图书馆，它的书籍包含所有可能的字母和数字的组合。每一部伟大的著作都在那里，你的死亡记录在那里，每一项可以写下来的可能的科学发现都在图书馆里。但任何真实的信息都会被隐藏在所有可能的虚假信息和完全胡言乱语中，变得无法区分。
这个故事是从1941年的一个短得多的故事--跷跷板发展而来的。反过来，它又被用作1951年修复小说《伊舍尔的武器商店》的一部分基础。在同一时期出版的另一部密切相关的小说是“武器制造者”。这些书围绕着不朽的罗伯特·海德罗克展开，他曾经创建了武器商店，作为对抗长期统治地球的帝国政府的一股力量。这两部小说比他的其他书更多地融合了范沃格特的硬科幻风格和超凡的超级英雄。然而，这篇短篇小说本身可能会做得更好。
帕吉特是夫妇亨利·库特纳(Henry Kuttner)和C·L·摩尔(C.L.Moore)在一起写作时的笔名，通常是为了幽默。这个故事标题的灵感来自刘易斯·卡罗尔的经典小说“透过镜子”中的一首诗“Jabberwocky”。它想象着神秘的教育玩具从未来悄悄进入，导致孩子们发现它们开始以新的模式思考和在圣彼得堡进行交流。
布朗还写侦探小说，他以科幻百科全书所称的“专业效应经济”所写的故事而闻名。这篇短篇小说就是其中之一。它讲述了通过一个人和一个外星人之间的单枪匹马来解决一场星际战争的故事。对于《星际迷航》的粉丝们来说，无论是故事名称还是剧情摘要都可能看起来很熟悉；这是经典系列第一季的热门剧集。
这个故事最初是以莱因斯特的真名(威廉·F·詹金斯饰)出版的，包含了小说中对计算机(称为逻辑)的早期描述。在故事中，他从很多方面想象了互联网。他设想每个家庭都有逻辑，通过分布式的服务器系统(称为坦克)连接起来，提供通信、娱乐、数据访问和商业服务；有一个人物说，逻辑就是文明。(#34；TANKS&34；TANKS&#34；TANKS&#34；TANKS)，提供通信、娱乐、数据访问和商业；有一个人物说，逻辑就是文明。
杰克逊以写悬疑和恐怖而闻名，而这个故事与通常被认为是科幻小说的故事背道而驰。(这篇报道发表在“纽约客”上，她的短篇作品很少出现在传统的科幻杂志上。)六月的彩票，玉米很快就会变重。说得太多可能会泄露太多关于这个强有力的短篇小说的信息。引用马丁·卡希尔(Martin Cahill)的话：如果你问任何人一个让他们一生难忘的美国短篇小说，如果他们想了一会儿，然后说，“那个故事，‘彩票’，”然后说，“那该死的狗屎。”我不会感到惊讶。她的许多最好的故事出现在彩票和其他故事集中。
有人可能会说，这个故事与波罗戈夫夫妇的米姆西有着相似的前提，在这个故事中，一个物体，在这个案例中，是一个医生的小黑包，被及时送回了原处。然而，只要说这不是一个同样有趣的故事就足够了。科恩布鲁斯的经典之作是这个故事及其续集“行军笨蛋”。然而，他是一位多产作家，他的其他作品包括“两个末日”(Two Doom)，这本书被认为是如果纳粹赢得二战治疗的最好作品之一。
布拉德伯里的作品有一种诗意和唤起人心的风格，可以像科幻小说一样倾向于幻想和恐怖。查理·简·安德斯(Charlie Jane Anders)后来出现在这份榜单上，他对这个特别的故事说了这样的话：#34；但在这个故事中，基本上是一座智能房屋在它的人类居民全部死亡后继续前进，在这场绞刀式的抽奖中，它是一个特殊的联盟。就像第二次世界大战后的许多故事一样，它关注的是核毁灭的威胁，也关注我们的技术可能比我们活得更久。这个故事出现在“火星编年史”中，这是布拉德伯里最伟大的作品，即使你不是布拉德伯里的狂热爱好者，你也应该读一读整本书。但是，如果你想在这一系列相关故事中寻找更多火星题材的故事，那就试试“第三次远征”吧。
在20世纪50年代，布利什写了他的奥基故事，设想有一天，地球上的城市会喜欢在星空中徘徊的移民。这些故事在范围上是史诗般的，最终被编成了一本单独的卷，名为“飞行中的城市”(Cities In Flight)。在同一时期，他也在写短篇小说，其中“表面张力”是我最喜欢的。这个故事讲述了在飞船坠毁在一个只有水坑的星球上后，一名宇宙飞船机组人员通过基因工程将他们的后代改造成可以生存的东西之后发生的事件。如果这些小型化的后代想要超越他们的单一水坑，他们必须克服表面张力。其他广受好评的布利什短篇小说包括“公共时间”。
这个故事比科幻小说更恐怖，它是原版《暮光之城》一集特别精彩的基础；它还引发了各种翻拍和即兴表演，包括《辛普森一家》(The Simpsons)的一集。故事的中心是安东尼·弗里蒙特(Anthony Fremont)，一个拥有近乎神的力量的三岁男孩；他可以把其他人或物体变成任何他想要的东西，把新事物变成现实，把自己和其他人传送到他想要的地方，读懂人和动物的思想，甚至可以复活死者。由于安东尼能读心术，镇上的居民不仅要对他身边的情况感到满意，而且要认为他们在任何时候都很快乐。
这是一个关于一个人和他的机器人的令人窒息的故事，他们的性格交织在一起，变成了一个疯狂而凶残的人格的两个方面。作家罗伯特·西尔弗伯格将其描述为故事结构的典范和活跃的风格，这才是它在这份崇高的榜单上脱颖而出的真正原因。其他特别值得注意的贝斯特短篇小说有“亚当与诺夏娃”和“谋杀穆罕默德的人”。
现在我们面临艰难的，至少有点武断的选择。克拉克是这份名单上的作家之一--另外两位，阿西莫夫和海因莱因很快就会出现--他们写出了数量惊人的高质量短篇小说，以至于只选一个真的是不可能的。但我们到了这一步。我向“星报”点头，因为它比克拉克的大部分作品都有更深刻的情感。关于这个故事很难说得太多而不泄露一些信息。这么说吧，它涉及的是一次探险，发现一个先进文明的遗迹在它的恒星发生超新星时被摧毁了。克拉克最喜欢的其他短裤还包括“上帝的90亿个名字”、“黑暗之墙”、“救援派对”和“与美杜莎的会面”。我可能还会加上“哨兵”，但主要是因为它是电影“2001：太空漫游”的灵感来源。
这个有点作弊。20世纪50年代初，米勒出版了一系列短篇小说，很快就被合并并改编成他唯一的一部小说，也是他广为人知的作品--“莱博维茨颂歌”。“莱博维茨颂歌”可以说是最好的后世界末日科幻作品，与更常见的“疯狂的麦克斯”模式有很大的不同。这部小说以大屠杀后数百年的黑暗时代为背景，从僧侣保存知识的角度讲述了它的故事，就像之前的黑暗时代一样。重新改编的菲亚特人构成了“颂歌”的第一部分，可以说是最强大的，但实际上，它是你将阅读的完整小说。
虽然不是特别出名(他从未获得过科幻小说写作奖)，但化名菲利普·克拉斯(Philip Klass)的田恩写的是机智的故事，通常带有黑色喜剧的一面。这个故事写在他活跃的写作生涯接近尾声的时候，背景设定在遥远的未来，在那里通过了一项法律，允许公民对他们打算犯下的罪行服刑，服刑完整，但犯罪前有50%的折扣。当两名这样的前科罪犯返回地球，打算实施他们应得的罪行时，事情就不会按计划进行了。布鲁克林项目是另一个值得你花时间的故事，它几乎是一个很难决定的故事。
阿西莫夫本人表示，这是他最喜欢的短篇小说之一。我一下子就想出了这个主意，不用摆弄它；我用白热化的方式写了出来，几乎不需要改动一个字。任何作家都喜欢这种东西，他曾经写过这样的故事。这真的是一个近乎完美的基于想法的科幻短篇小说。它讲述了一系列名为Multivac的计算机的发展，以及它们与人类的关系，通过七个历史背景的课程，从2061年地球成为行星文明的那一天开始。在前六个场景中，每个场景中都有一个不同的角色向计算机提出相同的问题，即如何避免宇宙的热死对人类生存构成的威胁。它很紧凑，很有趣，而且有一个杀手级的结局。他的原创中篇小说“夜幕降临”也很精彩，但我认为这部短篇小说会获得认可。他的一个或多个机器人故事也可以进入这个榜单，但我认为它们更像是一个集体成就奖，而不是个人杰出人物。
这个故事涉及时间旅行悖论，并进一步探索了他早期作品中由他的引导引入的主题。在对故事的分析中，达维·拉莫斯写道，个人主义以及自由表达爱情和性是其主题之一。这里分析的这部作品将因果关系的界限推到了令人费解的极端，以讨论性的界限和身份的逻辑。叙述流程很复杂，第一次你可能不会完全靠自己理解。电影“宿命”就是根据这个故事改编的。我还将其他一些短篇小说列入榜单，包括乔纳森·霍格(Jonathan Hoag)令人不快的职业，以及许多从过去到明天的未来历史收集的故事，包括安魂曲和“时间足够爱”的前传“玛土塞拉的孩子们”(Methuselah‘s Children)。(注：不愉快的职业是乔纳森·霍格(Jonathan Hoag)的不愉快职业)，还有许多从过去到明天收集的故事，包括“安魂曲”(Requiem)和“时间足够爱”的前传。
阿尔杰农是一只实验老鼠，他接受了提高智力的手术。这个故事由查理·戈登(Charlie Gordon)撰写的一系列进展报告讲述，他是该手术的第一个人类受试者，它触及了伦理和道德主题，如智障患者的治疗。这个短篇小说后来被扩展成长篇小说，并被拍成电影《查莉》(克里夫·罗伯逊因此获得奥斯卡最佳男主角)和许多其他改编，但我认为原著故事仍然是最有效的。
巴拉德最出名的可能是他的战争小说“太阳帝国”(不管人们是否知道他的名字)，这是一部关于一个年轻人的半自传体故事。
尽管冯内古特是一位主流作家，但不管这到底是什么意思，他的许多小说都包含了奇幻或科幻元素，无论是“猫的摇篮的冰九”、“第五屠宰场的时间位移”，还是几乎所有与“泰坦的塞伦斯”有关的东西。这个故事的前提是，宪法修正案规定，所有美国人都是完全平等的，不允许比其他任何人更聪明、更漂亮或更有身体能力。手提电脑总公司的代理人执行平等法，强迫公民佩戴残疾人面具，比如为太漂亮的人戴口罩，为聪明人耳朵里的思想打乱声音的收音机，为强壮或健壮的人佩戴重物。
这是读者自己发现的又一个故事。它涉及到“圣经”的传道书、语言、火星和预言，我们应该把它留在这里。这个故事是泽拉兹尼职业生涯的早期作品，他后来写了许多小说，最著名的是光之王和他的琥珀系列。
当我还是一名本科生的时候，我有幸看到埃里森讲了几次话，他很模拟。
.
note_en:
I've been playing around with re-reading favorite science fiction short stories while also seeing if there's anything that has managed to elude me over the years. I've also tried to catch up with more recent authors given that I don't read as many books as I used to. (The market for science fiction short stories, in general, is  in something of a decline but there are still some fabulous recent stories out there.)
   One story per author. Certainly there are many on this list for whom I could effortlessly reel off multiple deserving entries. A side effect of this rule is that it brought a fair bit of organic diversity to the list which would otherwise have been more heavily populated by a handful of favorite authors.
 Nothing is novel length but you'll find plenty of novelettes and novellas alongside the short stories.
 I also didn't include authors whose novels I enjoy but just don't have any short stories I'm aware of that wowed me.
 Everything here can be plausibly categorized as science fiction although some hew far closer to traditional sf tropes than others that are at least adjacent to horror, fantasy, and philosophical fiction.
 I used various lists and recommendations to tickle my memory and to ferret out works I might not be familiar with. But I've read everything here and don't think I've included anything just because it's "expected." These are personal favorites, or at least a coin flip among favorite stories by a given author. I did aim for some overall variety of time period, style, and subject matter but I think anything here can be enjoyed by a modern reader without making too many allowances.
  Many of these stories can be found online and others in popular "best of" collections.
  Anyway, my initial list (sorted chronologically) is below. While it's one story per author I do include pointers to other works in the comments. I've been careful to err on the side of avoiding spoilers but if you want to go into the stories completely cold, by all means, just work from this list of titles and authors.
    The earliest story on this list comes from prolific Victorian writer Herbert George Wells. Wells wrote in a variety of genres including social commentary and history. However, he's most remembered for his science fiction novels like  War of the Worlds (which was famously made into a radio play as well as films of varying levels of quality). The story used the fictional device of a man recounting dreams of a future time in which he is a political figure who has withdrawn from life to live with a younger woman on the island of Capri, even though this withdrawal is enabling opponents to start a great war with futuristic aircraft. Wells would later build on the concept for his novel  The Shape of Things to Come which would itself serve as the loose basis for a film of the same name in 1936.
    Rudyard Kipling would win the Novel Prize in Literature a couple years after this story was published. Unlike Wells, Kipling is not remembered for science fiction; rather he's mostly associated with stories and poetry often set in colonial India, where he was born and lived much of his early life. "With the Night Mail" is one of only two science fiction stories that Kipling wrote. Both concern the workings and activities of the The Aerial Board of Control, a 21st century fictional organization that manages the dirigible and other air traffic for the whole world and acts as a  de facto world government.
    Rounding out this trio of Victorian English authors, Forster's reputation also comes in part from a novel,  A Passage to India, that concerns the relationship between East and West as seen through the lens of India in the later days of the British Raj--as well as other novels that primarily focused on class differences in England. However, Forster wrote that "'The Machine Stops' is a reaction to one of the earlier heavens of H. G. Wells." [The Eloi of  The Time Machine.] In this novella, humanity lives underground and relies on a giant machine to provide its needs. There are technologies similar to video conferencing and the Internet. But the machine begins to fail and no one remembers how to fix it. This story is widely considered to be one of the most prescient short works of early science fiction.
    H.P. Lovecraft's primary reputation is as a horror writer. However, his stories that were part of the Cthulhu Mythos (such as this one) borrow premises often associated with science fiction such as alien invasion, other dimensions, and interference with human cultural and physiological evolution. For example, this novella popularized ancient astronaut theories as it details the events of a disastrous expedition to the Antarctic continent and what was found there by a group of explorers led by the narrator, Dr. William Dyer of Miskatonic University.
    Originally published in the magazine  Astounding Science Fiction, this story is an early example of the use of the 'pocket universe' concept in science fiction. A biochemist develops a synthetic life form which lives (and invents) far more quickly than humans can. This biochemist, Kidder, is their "god" who exerts his power over them and profits from them. Sturgeon is not generally very well known but this particular story regularly appears in collections and best of lists.
    This short story, originally written in Spanish by Argentine author and librarian Jorge Luis Borges, is  more of a philosophical exploration than a science fiction story in the usual sense. It asks you to imagine a library whose books contain every possible combination of letters and numbers. Every great work is there, the account of your death is there, every possible scientific discovery that can be written down exists in the library. But any true information would be buried in, and rendered indistinguishable from, all possible forms of false information and complete gibberish.
    This story was developed from a much shorter 1941 story, "The Seesaw". It was, in turn, used as the basis for a portion of the 1951 fix-up novel  The Weapon Shops of Isher. Another closely related novel published during the same period was  The Weapon Makers. These books revolve around the immortal Robert Hedrock who once created the Weapon Shops as a force to counteract the imperial world government long dominant on earth. These two novels bring together van Vogt's style of hard science fiction and transcendent superheroes more than his other books. However, this short story may do an even better job on its own.
    Padgett was the pseudonym of spouses Henry Kuttner and C. L. Moore when they were writing together, often to humorous effect. This story title was inspired by a verse from “Jabberwocky,” a poem found in the classic novel  Through the Looking-Glass by Lewis Carroll. It imagines mysterious educative toys timeslipped in from the future, which leads to the children discovering them to start thinking in new patterns and communicating in strange ways. "Twonky" and "The Time Locker" are other Padgett stories for which they're often remembered.
    Brown, who also wrote detective fiction, was noted for stories that were written with, what  The Encyclopedia of Science Fiction calls "professional economies of effect." This short story is one of those. It tells of the settling of an interstellar war through single combat between a human and an alien. Either the story name or the plot summary may seem familiar to Star Trek fans; it was a popular episode during the first season of the classic series.
    This story, originally published under Leinster's real name (William F. Jenkins) contains an early description of a computer (called a "logic") in fiction. In the story, he imagined the Internet in many respects. He envisioned logics in every home, linked through a distributed system of servers (called "tanks"), to provide communications, entertainment, data access, and commerce; one character says that "logics are civilization."
    Jackson was known for writing mystery and horror and this story strays as far as any on this list from what's usually considered science fiction. (This story was published in  The New Yorker and few of her short works were in traditional science fiction magazines.) "Lottery in June, corn be heavy soon." To say much more is to probably give too much about this powerful short story away.  To quote Martin Cahill: "If you asked anyone about a American short story that stuck with them for their entire lives, it would not shock me if they were to think for a moment, and then say, “that one story, ‘The Lottery,’” followed up with some form of, “that shit is fucked up.” Many of her best stories appear in the collection  The Lottery and Other Stories.
    One might say that this story shares a similar premise with "Mimsy were the Borogoves" in that an object, in this case, a doctor's little black bag, gets sent back in time. However, suffice it to say that this is not an equally playful story. Kornbluth's classic works are this story and its sequel "The Marching Morons." However, he was a prolific author and his other works include "Two Dooms" which is considered to be one of the better What if the Nazis Won WWII treatments.
    Bradbury's work has a poetic and evocative style that can tend towards fantasy and horror as much as science fiction. Charlie Jane Anders, who appears later on this list,  had this to say about this particular story: "But this one, in which basically a smart house keeps going after its human inhabitants are all dead, is in a special league in the knife-twisting sweepstakes. Like a lot of stories in the years following World War II, it's concerned with the threat of nuclear annihilation, but also with how our technology might outlive us." This story appears in  The Martian Chronicles, which is Bradbury's greatest work and you should just read the whole thing even if you're not a particular Bradbury enthusiast. But if you're looking for a single more Martian-themed story in that collection of linked stories, give "The Third Expedition" a try.
    During the 1950s, Blish wrote his Okie stories which envision a time when earthbound cities take to migrant wandering among the stars. Epic in scope, these stories were eventually wrangled into a single volume,  Cities in Flight. During the same period, he was writing short stories of which "Surface Tension" is my favorite. The story concerns the events that follow after a spaceship crew genetically engineering their descendants into something that can survive after the ship crashlanded on a planet with only puddles of water. The miniaturized descendants must overcome surface tension if they're to get beyond their single puddle. Other well-regarded Blish short stories include "Common Time."
    This story, more horror than science fiction, was the basis of a particularly good episode of the original Twilight Zone; it also has led to various remakes and riffs that played off of the idea including an episode of  The Simpsons. At the center of the story is Anthony Fremont, a three-year-old boy with near-godlike powers; he can transform other people or objects into anything he wishes, think new things into being, teleport himself and others where he wishes, read the minds of people and animals and even revive the dead. As Anthony can read minds, the town's population must not only act content with the situation when near him, but also think they are happy at all times.
    This is a breathless story of a man and his android whose personalities intermesh to become two aspects of a single insane murderous personality. Author Robert Silverberg has describes it as a "paragon of story construction and exuberant style" which is what really makes it stand out on this exalted list. Other Bester short stories of particular note are "Adam and No Eve" and "The Men Who Murdered Mohammed."
    Now we get into tough and at least somewhat arbitrary choices. Clarke is one of the authors on this list--two others, Asimov and Heinlein are coming up shortly--who produced such an astonishing volume of quality short stories that picking just one is really impossible. But here we are. I give "The Star" the nod because there's deeper emotion than in much of Clarke's work. It's hard to say too much about this story without giving things away. Let's just say it concerns an expedition that has discovered the remnants of an advanced civilization destroyed when its star went supernova. Other favorite Clarke shorts include "The Nine Billion Names of God," "The Wall of Darkness," "Rescue Party," and "Meeting with Medusa." I might also throw in "The Sentinel" but mostly because it was the inspiration for the film  2001: A Space Odyssey.
    This one is a bit of a cheat. In the early 1950s, Miller published a series of short stories which would shortly be combined and reworked into  A Canticle for Leibowitiz, his only novel and the work for which he is known.  A Canticle for Leibowitz is arguably the best post-apocalyptic work of science fiction and differs significantly from the more common "Mad Max" model. The novel is set in a sort of Dark Ages hundreds of years after the holocaust and tells its story from the point of view of monks preserving knowledge as in the previous Dark Ages. The reworked "Fiat Homo" makes up the first part of  Canticle and is arguably the strongest but, in practice, it's the full novel that you'll read.
  While not especially well-known (he never won a science fiction writing award), Tenn, the pseudonym of Philip Klass, wrote sharp-witted stories, often with a darkly comic side. This story, written fairly near the end of his active writing career, is set in a far future where a law has been passed enabling citizens to serve out sentences for crimes they intend to commit, serving the full term, but with a 50% pre-criminal discount. When two such pre-criminals return to earth intending to carry out the crimes they've earned, things don't go as planned. "The Brooklyn Project" is another story that well-worth your time and was pretty much a toss-up with this one.
    Asimov himself suggested that this was one of his favorite short stories. "I got the idea all at once and didn't have to fiddle with it; and I wrote it in white-heat and scarcely had to change a word. This sort of thing endears any story to any writer," he once wrote. It really is almost a perfect idea-based science fiction short story. It deals with the development of a series of computers called Multivac and their relationships with humanity through the courses of seven historic settings, beginning on the day in 2061 when Earth becomes a planetary civilization. In each of the first six scenes a different character presents the computer with the same question; namely, how the threat to human existence posed by the heat death of the universe can be averted. it's tight, it's funny, and it's got a killer ending. His original novella "Nightfall" is also wonderful but I think the short story gets the nod. One or more of his Robot stories could make their way onto this list as well but I think of them as more of a group achievement award than individual standouts.
    This story involves time travel paradoxes and further explores themes introduced in his earlier By His Bootstraps."  In his analysis of the story, Davi Ramos writes that "Individualism and the free expression of love and sexuality are among its main themes. The work analyzed here pushes the boundaries of causality to mind-bending extremes, in order to discuss the boundaries of sexuality and the logic of identity." The narrative flow is complex and you probably won't totally get it on your own the first time around. The film  Predestination is based on this story. Some other short stories I'd put on the list are "The Unpleasant Profession of Jonathan Hoag" and many of the stories collected in  The Past Through Tomorrow future history including "Requiem" and the prequel to  Time Enough for Love, "Methuselah's Children."
    Algernon is a laboratory mouse who has undergone surgery to increase his intelligence. The story is told by a series of progress reports written by Charlie Gordon, the first human subject for the surgery, and it touches on ethical and moral themes such as the treatment of the mentally disabled. The short story was later expanded into a novel and made into the film  Charly (for which Cliff Robertson won the Oscar for Best Actor) and many other adaptations but I think the original story is still the most effective.
    Ballard probably became best known (whether people knew his name or not) for his war novel,  Empire of the Sun, a semi-autobiographical account of a young British boy's experiences in Shanghai during Japanese occupation--which Stephen Spielberg later into a film. But he also had a career as a science fiction author which spanned the "world destroying" genre ( The Drowned World,  The Wind from Nowhere) like other British authors such as John Wyndham to experimental efforts which were adapted into films like  David Cronenberg's  Crash. However, he also wrote some enticing short stories. One of them is "Chronopolis" which begins with a man in prison, Newman, and proceeds to examine his fascination with the concept of time in a world where clocks have been prohibited and are regulated by time police.
    The nom de plume of Paul Myron Anthony Linebarger, Smith (not to be confused with E. E. Doc Smith of Lensman fame) wrote relatively little, in part because he held jobs that required extensive foreign travel. Most of his science fiction was in the Instrumentality Universe and author Frederik Pohl described his stories as "a wonderful and inimitable blend of a strange, raucous poetry and a detailed technological scene, we begin to read of human beings in worlds so far from our own in space in time that they were no longer quite Earth." This story is one of his more lyrical works. It's adjacent in timeline to "Scanners Live in Vain" which is another Smith story written ten years earlier near the beginning of his science fiction writing. While Scanners is often cited as Smith's best work, I find its older provenance give it a more archaic feel.
    Although Vonnegut was a mainstream author, whatever that means exactly, many of his novels contained fantastical or science fictional elements whether the Ice Nine of  Cat's Cradle, the time displacement of  Slaughterhouse Five, or just about anything related to  The Sirens of Titan. The premise of this story is that amendments to the Constitution dictate that all Americans are fully equal and not allowed to be smarter, better-looking, or more physically able than anyone else. The Handicapper General's agents enforce the equality laws, forcing citizens to wear "handicaps" such as masks for those who are too beautiful, loud radios that disrupt thoughts inside the ears of intelligent people, and heavy weights for the strong or athletic.
    This is another one of those stories that the reader is probably best discovering on their own. It involves the Biblical book of  Ecclesiastes, language, Mars, and prophesy and we should probably leave it at that. This story was an early career work for Zelazny who would go on to write many novels, most notably  Lord of Light and his Amber series.
    I had the pleasure of seeing Ellison speak a couple of times when I was an undergrad and he was sim
......
title: 50位作家，50个科幻故事比小说短
title_en: 50 authors, 50 science fiction stories shorter than novels

在艺术家弗朗斯·斯奈德斯(Frans Snyders)的巴洛克杰作水果摊(Fruit Stall)里，一大张木桌上，一排令人印象深刻的农产品散落在篮子和盘子里。有些商品一眼就能认出：例如，从桌子中央的一个大篮子里溢出来的绿葡萄。但其他商品，包括一种裂开的绿色水果，点缀着黑色的种子，现代人不太熟悉。
几年前，植物遗传学家艾夫·德·斯梅特(Ive De Smet)和艺术历史学家大卫·韦尔戈文(David Verguwen)在参观圣彼得堡的国立隐士博物馆(State Hermitage Museum)时对水果摊进行了第一手研究。德斯梅特告诉CNN的克里斯汀·罗杰斯(Kristen Rogers)，在观看这幅作品时，两人都意识到，两人都无法辨认出场景中描绘的一些水果。最初，这位科学家推测，专门绘制以水果、蔬菜和动物为特色的静物画的斯奈德斯缺乏天赋。
德斯梅特在一份声明中说：“但(韦尔戈文)告诉我，这实际上是17世纪最好的画家之一。”所以，如果水果是这样描述的，那么它看起来也应该是这样的。“。
这场讨论激发了朋友们开始一项非传统的研究冒险，这项研究最近发表在“植物科学趋势”(Trends In Plant Science)杂志上。通过将现代植物遗传学与几个世纪以来的静物画相结合，研究人员意识到他们可以创造出一条产品驯化的视觉时间表。现在，他们希望众包一个相关艺术品的图书馆，以便分析更广泛的来源。
“我们开始做一些挖掘，我想我们从来没有停止过挖掘，”德斯梅特告诉zme Science的提比·普尤(Tibi Puiu)。“有些朋友一起打网球，或者去钓鱼。艾维和大卫参观博物馆，会见其他学者，看画，研究我们现代食物的历史。“。
研究中引用的一些最古老的作品来自古埃及，那里的艺术家描绘了带有深绿色和浅绿色条纹的西瓜，与今天看到的类似。Verguwen和De Smet在2019年的一篇论文中写道，对在埃及坟墓中发现的西瓜叶的遗传分析表明，这种现代水果的数千年前的前身尝起来像黄瓜。
根据这项研究，对农产品的艺术描绘很有用，因为它们可以揭示人类将野生植物驯服成美味的一步一步的过程，以及某些食物何时出现在世界不同地区。
德斯梅特向CNN解释说：“我们主要对这个故事感兴趣，比如说，现代橙色胡萝卜从一开始只是一种杂草，到现在的流行形式。”“古代植物性食物的基因组可以帮助我们了解这种植物可能是什么样子--例如，基于产生不同颜色的活动途径的颜色--以及它可能具有的特征--例如，甜度。”这有助于我们准确地确定特定特征在时间线上的外观，就像绘画可以做到的那样。“
ART允许研究人员追踪西红柿等食物走过的道路，西红柿在16世纪被担心有毒，但在19世纪成为意大利厨房的主食。2015年，巴里·埃斯塔布鲁克为史密森杂志报道，西红柿起源于秘鲁，今天仍然可以在那里找到这种栽培植物的野生祖先。
在意大利翁布里亚地区，农学家伊莎贝拉·达拉·拉吉奥内(Isabella Dalla Ragione)正在带头利用文艺复兴时期的绘画重新发现丢失的水果和蔬菜。正如维多利亚·特拉弗索(VitVictoria Traverso)在2月份为Atlas Obscura所报道的那样，达拉·加吉奥内(Dalla Gagione)在废弃的田野中寻找结有被遗忘水果。许多人在转向工业化的过程中落在了后面，因为他们生长得太慢，或者不能用机械收获。相比之下，德斯梅特和韦尔格温研究的食物往往是现代品种的祖先。
研究人员在论文中指出，所有对食物的描述都受到艺术许可的限制。例如，斯奈德的市场场景描绘了比一个季节可能收获的水果种类更多的水果。一些现代艺术品-比如巴勃罗·毕加索(Pablo Picasso)的“水果碗、小提琴和瓶子”-太抽象了，以至于它们对食物的实际外观几乎没有提供什么见解。
乔治亚大学农业与环境科学学院的植物生物学家埃斯特·范德克纳普(Esther Van Der Knaap)没有参与这项研究，他告诉CNN：“通过绘画和其他艺术形式进行搜索当然是一种追求的途径。”虽然历史艺术并不完美，但它提供了对几个世纪前重要特征的额外洞察力。
为了衡量一位特定的画家对其主题的描绘有多准确，德斯梅特和韦尔戈文使用玫瑰作为参照点。这些花已经被驯化了大约5000年，这种植物走向驯化的步骤已经有了很好的记录。
用艺术来追踪水果和蔬菜的进化也有它自己的一系列挑战。正如美国有线电视新闻网(CNN)所指出的那样，博物馆藏品的在线目录通常“只提供某些艺术品的简短标题和描述以及小照片”，因此很难容易地识别相关的画作。此外，许多潜在的重要艺术品都存放在研究人员无法亲自参观的私人收藏品中。
德斯梅特告诉zme：“如果西班牙修道院的厨房里描绘了一个有趣的17世纪西红柿，而这座修道院几乎从不向游客开放，那么我们就有可能永远不会发现这一点。”“这就是我们需要帮助的原因。”
目前，那些希望为#ArtGenetics项目做出贡献的人必须直接给作者发电子邮件。但据Artnet News的莎拉·卡斯科内(Sarah Cascon)称，一款应用程序和一个提交材料的公共数据库正在筹备中。
“这就是今天做这类研究的美妙之处，”德斯梅特对CNN说。“众包工具将允许你以比我们仅仅参观博物馆更快的速度访问更多的数据。”
note_en:
In   Fruit Stall, a Baroque masterpiece by artist Frans Snyders, an impressive array of produce appears strewn across baskets and platters on a large wooden table. Some of the items on offer are instantly recognizable: Take, for instance, the green grapes overflowing from a large basket at the center of the table. But other goods, including a split-open green fruit dotted with black seeds, are less familiar to the modern eye.
    Plant geneticist  Ive De Smet and art historian  David Vergauwen studied  Fruit Stall firsthand during a visit to the State  Hermitage Museum in St. Petersburg several years ago. While viewing the work, the pair realized that neither could identify some of the fruits depicted in the scene, De Smet tells  CNN’s Kristen Rogers. Initially, the scientist theorized that  Snyders, who specialized in still-life paintings featuring fruit, vegetables and animals, lacked talent.
  “But [Vergauwen] told me this was actually one of the best painters from the 17th century,” says De Smet in a  statement.” So, if that’s how the fruit was depicted, that’s how it should look.”
  This discussion inspired the friends to embark on an unconventional research venture newly cataloged in the journal   Trends in Plant Science. By combining modern plant genetics with centuries of still-life paintings, the researchers realized that they could create a visual timeline of produce domestication. Now, they hope to  crowdsource a library of relevant artworks in order to analyze a wider breadth of sources.
  “We started to do some digging and I guess we’ve never stopped digging,” De Smet tells  ZME Science’s Tibi Puiu. “Some friends play tennis together or go fishing. Ive and David visit museums, meet other scholars, look at paintings and study the history of our modern foods.”
    Some of the oldest works cited in the study come from ancient Egypt, where artists depicted watermelons with dark and light green stripes similar to those seen today. Genetic analysis of a watermelon leaf found in an Egyptian tomb suggests the modern fruit’s millennia-old predecessor tasted like cucumbers, wrote Vergauwen and De Smet in a  2019 paper.
  Artistic portrayals of produce are useful because they can reveal the step-by-step processes through which humans tamed wild plants into something delicious, as well as when certain foods appeared in different parts of the world, according to the study.
  “We are mainly interested in the story that, say, the modern orange carrot made from its humble beginnings as a weed, to its current popular form,” De Smet explains to CNN. “Genomes of ancient plant-based foods can help us understand what this plant could have looked like—for example, color based on the active pathways that produce different colors—and which characteristics it might have possessed—for example, sweetness. This helps us pinpoint the appearance of certain characteristics on a timeline, the same way paintings can.”
  Art allows researchers to trace the paths traveled by such foods as tomatoes, which were feared to be poisonous during the 16th century but became a staple in 19th-century Italian kitchens. Tomatoes originated in Peru, and the cultivated plant’s wild ancestor can still be found there today, reported Barry Estabrook for   Smithsonian magazine in 2015.
    In Italy’s Umbria region, agronomist  Isabella Dalla Ragione is spearheading similar efforts by using Renaissance paintings to rediscover lost fruits and vegetables. As Vittoria Traverso reported for   Atlas Obscura in February, Dalla Gagione searches abandoned fields for plants bearing forgotten fruits. Many were left behind during the shift to industrialization because they grew too slowly or couldn’t be harvested with machinery. Comparatively, the foods studied by De Smet and Vergauwen are often ancestors of modern varieties.
  All depictions of food are subject to artistic license, the researchers note in the paper. Snyders’ market scenes, for instance, depict a wider variety of fruit than could possibly be harvested in a single season. And some modern artworks—consider Pablo Picasso’s   Bowl of Fruit, Violin and Bottle—are so abstract that they offer few insights on foods’ actual appearance.
  “Searching through paintings and other art forms is certainly an avenue of pursuit,”  Esther van der Knaap, a plant biologist at the University of Georgia’s College of Agricultural and Environmental Sciences who wasn’t involved in the study, tells CNN. “While imperfect, historical art offers additional insights into the traits that were important centuries ago.”
  To gauge how accurately a given painter portrays their subject, De Smet and Vergauwen use roses as a reference point. The flowers have been domesticated for around 5,000 years, and the steps the plant took toward domestication are well-documented.
    Using art to track fruits and vegetables’ evolution comes with its own set of challenges. As CNN notes, online catalogs of museums’ collections often “offer only brief titles and descriptions and small photos of certain artworks,” making it difficult to easily identify relevant paintings. Additionally, many potentially crucial artworks are housed in private collections that the researchers are unable to visit in person.
  “If an interesting 17th-century tomato is depicted in the kitchen of a Spanish monastery that is almost never open to visitors, we run the risk of never finding out about that,” De Smet tells ZME. “That is why we need help.”
  For now, those hoping to contribute to the  #ArtGenetics project must email the authors directly. But an app and a public database of submissions are in the works, according  to   artnet News’ Sarah Cascone.
  “This is the beauty of doing this kind of research today,” De Smet says to CNN. “Crowdsourcing tools will allow you to access a lot more data faster than we ever could by just visiting museums.”
title: 几个世纪的古老绘画帮助研究人员追踪食物进化
title_en: Centuries-Old Paintings Help Researchers Track Food Evolution

今年，人们可能无法亲临圣地亚哥动漫展，但虚拟大会“动漫展@家”(Comic-Con@Home)整个周末都在举行，有无数的小组讨论、先行预告，以及即将上映的电视剧的预告片和预告片-但电影并不多，因为老实说：秋季上映的主要院线电影看起来不太好。仅在周四，我们就看到了“比尔和泰德直面后果”的完整预告片，西蒙·佩格/尼克·弗罗斯特合作的恐怖喜剧“真相探索者”的预告片，以及HBO电视台“他的黑暗材料”S2的首支预告片。我们没有继续单独报道每一个预告片，而是决定将剩下的感兴趣的预告片汇编成一个单独的综合帖子。
HBO公布了即将播出的恐怖连续剧“洛夫克拉夫特乡村”的最终预告片，并正式公布了上映日期：8月16日。该剧改编自马特·拉夫2016年的同名黑暗奇幻/恐怖小说“洛夫克拉夫特”，该小说明确讲述了20世纪50年代种族主义的恐怖，以及其他更具超自然色彩的洛夫克拉夫特式的问题。根据官方的提纲：
这部电视剧讲述了阿提克斯(乔纳森·梅格斯饰)与他的朋友利蒂夏(于尔尼·斯莫列特-贝尔饰)和他的叔叔乔治(考特尼·B·万斯饰)一起开始了一次穿越20世纪50年代吉姆·克罗美国的公路旅行，寻找他失踪的父亲(迈克尔·肯尼斯·威廉姆斯饰)。这开始了一场生存和克服美国白人的种族主义恐怖和可能从洛夫克拉夫特平装书上撕下的可怕怪物的斗争。
HBO在5月份发布了预告片，随后在6月份发布了完整的预告片。这支最新的预告片结合了之前的一些镜头，但为我们提供了更多的故事线索：即阿提卡斯的搜索涉及一个秘密的继承权，与一个富裕家庭在名义上的洛夫克拉夫特乡村深处的遗产有关，他无视一再发出的警告，要求他远离。之前的偷窥集中在种族主义催生的人类怪物上；现在这些洛夫克拉夫式的生物终于准备好了他们的特写镜头。这支新预告片让我们更加期待下个月的剧集首播。
这部新的亚马逊Prime系列是2013-2014年备受争议的英国黑色喜剧/阴谋惊悚片的重启(由Gone Girl和Sharp Objects作者Gillian Flynn改编)，这部电影讲述的是一部名为乌托邦的反乌托邦漫画小说的在线粉丝，这部小说似乎有能力预测现实世界的未来。他们痴迷于追踪续集(据推测，续集还可以预测未来的世界事件)。这使得他们成为一个名为网络的秘密组织的目标。英国版因其原创性和视觉风格而受到批评，但对其极端暴力的强烈保留抵消了这一点，许多人认为这是不必要的。(最著名的场景是一个施虐者用勺子挖出受害者的眼睛)。
亚马逊的乌托邦能否与同样规模的暴力相匹敌还有待观察，尽管我敢打赌，任何在“改变的碳”第一季中观看过延长刑讯逼供场面的人都应该能够应对。根据官方的假设：当难以捉摸的漫画乌托邦中的阴谋是真实的时，一群年轻的粉丝聚集在一起，开始一场高风险的扭曲冒险，利用他们发现的东西来拯救自己，拯救彼此，最终拯救人类。演员阵容包括约翰·库萨克(Grosse Pointe Blank)饰演凯文·克里斯蒂(Dr.Kevin Christie)，雷恩·威尔逊(Rainn Wilson)饰演迈克尔·斯蒂恩斯(Michael Stearns)，以及萨莎·莱恩(Sasha Lane，2019&39；饰演地狱男孩)。
如果威胁是真的，那就不是妄想症。这是约翰·斯莱特里(“广告狂人”、“聚焦”)即将上映的科技惊悚片“NeXT”的口号。根据官方的提纲：
接下来是一部基于事实的惊悚片，讲述了一种致命的流氓人工智能的出现，它将行动与对技术如何入侵我们的生活并以我们尚不了解的方式改变我们的研究相结合。斯莱特里饰演一位硅谷先驱，他发现自己的一个创意--强大的人工智能--可能会带来全球灾难，于是他与第一个创意中的费尔南达·安德拉德(Fernanda Andrade)饰演的网络犯罪特工联手打击恶棍。
预告片以硅谷企业家保罗·勒布朗(Slattery)类似TED的演讲开场，警告人类级别的人工智能的危险。切换到像亚历克西斯一样的人工智能助手伊莱扎(Eliza)正在与一个小男孩进行对话。伊莱扎没有问问题，她只是回答，男孩的父亲说，但在这种情况下，他错了。勒布朗的咆哮听起来越来越偏执，因为我们看到对面部识别、自动驾驶汽车和各种电子系统(包括医疗设备)的认可，所有这些似乎都在一种名为NeXT的新人工智能的控制之下，但它并不像它的创建者想象的那样良性。老实说，这让我想起了1993年的“X档案”那集“机器里的幽灵”--尤其是电梯致死的场景--只不过有更多公开的间谍元素。这并不令人惊讶：这部电视剧是由曼尼·库托(Manny Coto)创作的(24：Legacy)。
2019年，Hulu宣布开发两个以漫威为中心的新系列，《幽灵骑士》(加布里埃尔·露娜重新出演神盾局特工的角色)。还有“赫尔斯特罗姆”，它打算开启一部独立的“恐惧冒险”系列，为漫威公式带来令人不寒而栗的恐怖元素。《幽灵骑士》很快就半途而废，到了2019年12月，漫威电视台被关闭。这使得赫尔斯特罗姆成为计划中的基于恐惧的特许经营权的唯一幸存者。
该系列聚焦于漫威漫画中的两个角色：撒旦之子戴蒙·赫尔斯特罗姆(Daimon Hellstrom)，他在1973年的“幽灵骑士#1”(Ghost Rider#1)中被介绍，最终成为“捍卫者”(The Defender)中反复出现的角色。他的妹妹萨塔娜(电视改编版中的安娜饰)拥护神秘和她的父亲遗产，但戴蒙选择捍卫人类。根据官方假设：这个世界还没有为赫尔斯特罗姆一家团聚做好准备。作为一个神秘而强大的连环杀手的儿子和女儿，赫尔斯特罗姆跟随戴蒙(汤姆·奥斯汀饰)和安娜·赫尔斯特罗姆(悉尼·莱蒙饰)，以及他们复杂的动态，追查人类最邪恶的一面-每个人都有自己的态度和技能。
从音调上讲，这款预告片与经常推迟的“新变种人”(The New Mutants)(仍计划于8月28日上映；开场场面在Comic-Con@home上放映)是一致的，这是为超级英雄类型带来恐怖元素的另一次尝试。除了奥斯汀(“皇室”、格兰切斯特)和莱蒙(“天鹅绒Buzzsaw”、“行尸走肉”)外，该剧还将由伊丽莎白·漫威(“国土安全”、“纸牌屋”)饰演戴蒙和安娜的母亲维多利亚，维多利亚已经被收容了20年；罗伯特·威斯多姆(“火线”)饰演与恶魔搏斗的神秘守护者；琼·卡里尔(“心灵猎人”)饰演露西·黑斯廷斯。
自2009年首映以来，这部关于一个功能失调的情报机构的功绩的滑稽、不敬、非常元的詹姆斯·邦德(James Bond)的恶作剧一直是一件令人愉快的事情。在过去的几季里，这部剧采用了一些选集的形式--主要是因为它的主人公斯特林·阿彻(由H·乔恩·本杰明配音)一直处于昏迷状态，每一季的事件都发生在他的潜意识里。因此，S8被称为弓箭手梦境，核心演员成为1947年洛杉矶黑色背景下的人物，而S9，弓箭手：危险之岛，发生在1939年左右的一个偏远的南太平洋岛屿上。S10，弓箭手：1999，带领每个人进入外层空间，与赏金猎人和星际海盗作战。
阿切尔最终在S10决赛中醒来，为恢复正常运营铺平了道路--除了在阿切尔昏迷期间，世界已经在继续前进，他将不得不学习如何应对。根据官方的提纲：阿切尔醒了……他需要喝一杯。斯特林·阿切尔在昏迷了三年后准备重返间谍世界。虽然在他缺席期间很多事情都发生了变化，但阿切尔相信他只需要一点时间就能把事情重新恢复到原来的方式。问题是：团队的其他成员希望这样吗？其他人可能还没有为他的归来做好准备，不会在他们润滑得很好的机器上扔扳手。
自白：在S2之后，我失去了对《行尸走肉》的记忆，但这部僵尸剧仍然很强劲，并成为真正的特许经营，成功的衍生系列和三部据称正在拍摄中的电影。AMC在10月4日播出了“行尸走肉”(S10)大结局加长公映的预告片，并于10月6日首播了“行尸走肉”S6的预告片。10月4日也将是第三部衍生剧“行尸走肉：超越世界”(The Walking Dead：World Beyond)的首播，这部电视剧将限量播出两季。“行尸走肉：超越世界”(The Walking Dead：World Beyond)将于10月4日播出。从预告片来看，它看起来像是这个虚构世界里真正新鲜的镜头。故事背景设定在僵尸启示录十年后的内布拉斯加州，剧情聚焦在这个新时代中成年的两姐妹。官方提纲如下：
“行尸走肉：超越世界”探讨了第一个GE之后的新神话和新故事。
我们的年轻主人公在预告片中说，十年过去了，我们的逝者仍然拥有这个世界。幸存的人类似乎躲藏在被围墙隔开的社区里，而不死部落则在外面人类文明的废墟中漫步。和所有的青少年一样，姐妹们和她们的朋友们都想要一个更好的未来。另外，他们的父亲似乎有危险。当我们第一次看到四人组在他们安全的避风港外冒险时，画外音说，我们必须勇敢地度过我们现在所拥有的生活，仅仅是为了生存。仍然有僵尸对抗和大量的行动，但总体基调几乎是哀悼的，甚至是充满希望的，因为青少年们试图让我们的生命变得有价值，不是因为我们是最后一代，而是因为我们是开始。
note_en:
People might not be able to flock to San Diego Comic Con this year in person, but the virtual convention, Comic-Con@Home, has been running all weekend, with countless panels, sneak peeks, and teasers and trailers for upcoming TV shows—but not many films, because let's be honest: it's not looking so good for major theatrical film releases in the fall. On Thursday alone, we got the full trailer for   Bill and Ted Face the Music, a teaser for the Simon Pegg/Nick Frost horror comedy   Truth Seekers, and the first trailer for S2 of HBO's   His Dark Materials. Rather than continue to cover each individually, we decided to compile the remaining trailers of interest into a single roundup post.
   HBO unveiled the final trailer for its upcoming horror series,   Lovecraft Country, along with an official release date: August 16. It's based on the 2016 dark fantasy/horror novel  of the same name by Matt Ruff, which deals explicitly with the horrors of racism in the 1950s, along with other, more supernatural Lovecraftian-inspired issues. Per the official synopsis:
 The series follows Atticus (Jonathan Majors) as he joins up with his friend Letitia (Jurnee Smollett-Bell) and his Uncle George (Courtney B. Vance) to embark on a road trip across 1950s Jim Crow America in search of his missing father (Michael Kenneth Williams). This begins a struggle to survive and overcome both the racist terrors of white America and the terrifying monsters that could be ripped from a Lovecraft paperback.
 HBO released  a teaser in May, followed by a  full trailer in June. This latest trailer combines some of that prior footage, but gives us a few more hints of the story arc: namely, that Atticus' search involves a "secret birthright" relating to a rich family's estate deep in the titular Lovecraft Country, and that he ignores repeated warnings to stay away. Prior sneak peeks have focused on the human monsters spawned by racism; now the Lovecraftian creatures are finally ready for their closeup. This new trailer makes us even more eager for the series premiere next month.
   This new Amazon Prime series is a reboot (adapted by  Gone Girl and  Sharp Objects author Gillian Flynn) of the controversial 2013-2014 British black comedy/conspiracy thriller about online fans of a dystopian graphic novel called  Utopia that seems to have the power to predict the real-world future. They are obsessed with tracking down the sequel (which supposedly also predicts future world events). This makes them targets of a secret organization called The Network. The British version received critical praise for its originality and visual style, offset by strong reservations about its extreme violence, which struck many as  unnecessarily gratuitous. (The most famous scene involved a torturer using a spoon to gouge out a victim's eye).
 It remains to be seen if Amazon's   Utopia will match the same scale of violence, although I'd wager anyone who sat through the extended torture scenes in the  first season of   Altered Carbon should be handle to handle it. Per the official premise: "When the conspiracy in the elusive comic  Utopia is real, a group of young fans come together to embark on a high-stakes twisted adventure to use what they uncover to save themselves, each other and ultimately humanity." The cast includes John Cusack ( Grosse Pointe Blank) as Dr. Kevin Christie, Rainn Wilson ( The Office) as Michael Stearns, and Sasha Lane (2019's  Hellboy) as Jessica Hyde.
   "It’s not paranoia if the threat is real."  That's the tagline for   NeXT, an upcoming techno-thriller starring John Slattery ( Mad Men, Spotlight). Per the official synopsis:
 NeXT is a fact-based thriller about the emergence of a deadly, rogue artificial intelligence that combines action with an examination of how technology is invading our lives and transforming us in ways we don’t yet understand. Slattery stars as a Silicon Valley pioneer, who discovers that one of his own creations—a powerful A.I.—might spell global catastrophe and teams up with a cybercrime agent, played by  The First’s Fernanda Andrade, to fight a villain.
 The trailer opens with a TED-like talk by Silicon Valley entrepreneur Paul LeBlanc (Slattery) warning of the dangers of human-level AI. Cut to an Alexis-like AI assistant, Eliza, carrying on a conversation with a young boy. "Eliza doesn't ask questions, she just answers them," the boy's father says, but in this case, he's wrong. LeBlanc's rantings sound increasingly paranoid, as we see nods to facial recognition, self-driving cars, and various electronic systems (including medical devices) that all seem to come under the control of a new AI called NeXT that isn't as benign as its creators assume. Honestly, it reminds me of the 1993  The X-Files episode " Ghost in the Machine"—especially the death-by-elevator scene—only with more overt espionage elements. That's not surprising: the series was created by Manny Coto ( 24: Legacy).
   In 2019, Hulu announced the development of two new Marvel-centric series,  Ghost Rider (with Gabriel Luna reprising his role from  Agents of S.H.I.E.L.D.) and  Helstrom, intended to kick off a standalone " Adventure Into Fear" franchise that would bring a chilling horror element to the Marvel formula.  Ghost Rider soon fell by the wayside, and by December 2019, Marvel Television was shut down. That makes   Helstrom the sole survivor of the planned fear-based franchise.
 The series focuses on two characters from Marvel Comics: Daimon Hellstrom, the son of Satan, introduced in  Ghost Rider #1 (1973), who eventually became a recurring character in  The Defenders. His sister, Satana (Ana in the TV adaptation) embraces the occult and her paternal heritage, but Daimon chooses to defend humanity.  Per the official premise: "The world isn’t ready for a Helstrom family reunion. As the son and daughter of a mysterious and powerful serial killer, Helstrom follows Daimon (Tom Austen) and Ana Helstrom (Sydney Lemmon), and their complicated dynamic, as they track down the worst of humanity — each with their own attitude and skills."
 Tonally, the trailer is in line with the oft-delayed   The New Mutants ( still scheduled for an August 28 release; the opening scene was shown at Comic-Con@home), another attempt to bring elements of horror to the superhero genre. In addition to Austen ( The Royals, Grantchester) and Lemmon ( Velvet Buzzsaw, Fear the Walking Dead), the series will feature Elizabeth Marvel ( Homeland, House of Cards) as Daimon and Ana's mother, Victoria, who has been institutionalized for 20 years; Robert Wisdom ( The Wire) as Caretaker, a demon-fighting guardian of the occult; June Carryl ( Mindhunter) as Lousie Hastings, head of the psychiatric institution housing Victoria; and Ariana Guerra ( Raising Dion) as Vatican agent Gabriella Rossetti.
   This hilariously irreverent, very meta James Bond spoof about the exploits of a dysfunctional intelligence agency has been a delight ever since it premiered way back in 2009. It's taken on a bit of the anthology format for the past few seasons—mostly because its main protagonist, Sterling Archer (voiced by H. Jon Benjamin), has been in a coma, with the seasons' events all taking place in his subconscious. So S8 was known as  Archer Dreamland, with the core cast becoming characters in a 1947-era Los Angeles  noir setting, while S9,  Archer: Danger Island, took place around 1939 on a remote South Pacific island. S10,  Archer: 1999, took everyone into outer space, battling bounty hunters and intergalactic pirates.
 Archer finally woke up in the S10 finale, paving the way for return to normal operations—except the world has moved on without Archer during his coma and he's going to have to learn to cope. Per the official synopsis: "Archer is awake....and he needs a drink. Sterling Archer is ready to return to the spy world after a three-year coma. While many things changed during his absence, Archer is confident it will take just a little time for him to reset things back to the old ways. The problem: does the rest of the team want that? The others may not be ready for his return to throw a wrench in their well-oiled machine."
   Confession: I lost track of   The Walking Dead after S2, but the zombie drama is still going strong and becoming a bona fide franchise, with a successful spinoff series and three films purportedly in the works. AMC debuted a  sneak peek of the extended opening of the S10 finale, airing October 4, as well as  a teaser for S6 of   Fear The Walking Dead, premiering October 6. October 4 will also be the premiere of a third spinoff series,   The Walking Dead: World Beyond, designed to be a two-season limited run. And judging by the trailer, it looks like a genuinely fresh take within this fictional world. Set in Nebraska ten years after the zombie apocalypse, the plot focuses on two sisters who came of age in this new era. Per the official synopsis:
 The Walking Dead: World Beyond delves into a new mythology and story that follows the first generation raised in a surviving civilization of the post-apocalyptic world. Two sisters along with two friends leave a place of safety and comfort to brave dangers, known and unknown, living and undead on an important quest. Pursued by those who wish to protect them and those who wish to harm them, a tale of growing up and transformation unfurls across dangerous terrain, challenging everything they know about the world, themselves and each other. Some will become heroes. Some will become villains. But all of them will find the truths they seek.
 "We're ten years in now, and the dead still have this world," our young protagonist says in the trailer. The surviving humans appear to be holed up in walled-off communities, while the undead hordes roam the ruins of human civilization outside. And like all teens, the sisters and their friends want a better future. Plus, it seems their father is in danger. "We have to be brave in this life we have, simply to exist now," a voiceover says as we see the foursome venture outside the walls of their safe haven for the first time. There are still zombie confrontations and plenty of action, but the overall tone is almost elegiac, even hopeful, as the teens try to "make our lives count, not because we're the last generation—but because we're the beginning."

note: 硅芯片，几年前我们大多数人都没有注意到，现在每个人都听说过，很少人理解，很多人开始害怕。它真的是工业革命那样深刻的革命的基础吗？伯纳德·福克(Bernard Falk)以门外汉的观点看待硅芯片是什么，它是如何制造的，以及它是如何工作的。从芝加哥的百万富翁的家到伯明翰的汽车测试赛道，从苏格兰的一家精神病院到瑞典的一家工厂，他着眼于这项新技术是如何被使用的，以及它在未来可以做些什么。电影编辑约翰·莱特，制片人大卫·艾伦。
note_en: The silicon chip, unnoticed by most of us a few years ago, is now something everybody has heard about, few understand and many are beginning to fear. Is it really the basis for a revolution as profound as the industrial revolution? Bernard Falk takes a layman's view of what the silicon chip is, how it's made and works. From a Chicago millionaire's home to a Birmingham car-test track, and from a psychiatric hospital in Scotland to a Swedish factory, he looks at how the new technology is being used and what it could do in the future. Film editor JOHN LYTE Producer DAVID ALLEN.

note:
在过去的几十年里，气温上升的速度比过去2000年中的任何时候都要快。我们最近经历了有记录以来最热的五月，大气中的二氧化碳水平达到了前所未有的高度。今天，我们测试自然栖息地极限的结果正在上演，并准备加速并成倍增长。
一季接着一季，气候变化的社会损失是由与气候有关的自然灾害造成的。这些事件的频率和变化性超出了我们减轻其来源--温室气体排放--的能力，也超出了我们适应高风险的能力。
气候变化现在就在这里。2018年，全球自然灾害造成1600亿美元的经济损失。美国海湾或日本海岸的毁灭性风暴在两年内造成了超过6500亿美元的生命损失和经济损失。亚太地区的全球人口流离失所人数创下新高，仅2013年和2015年就有6000万人流离失所。到2030年，预计将有近10亿人居住在这个地区。
美国国防部称气候变化是“威胁倍增”。没有一个国家不受其影响。这一点在2011年叙利亚内战期间没有得到更明确的例证。该地区的一系列干旱导致年轻人大规模迁移到该国的城市。已经存在的社会经济压力因涌入而加剧。东非降雨量减少和长期干旱导致对自然资源的进一步根深蒂固的冲突，也代表了人为气候变化如何助长冲突的火上浇油。
五角大楼去年的一项研究警告了气候变化、战争和移民之间的联系-证实了全球气温上升已经并将加剧冲突和难民。几个月后，白宫国家安全委员会(White House National Security Council)成员罗德·斯库诺弗(Rod Schoonover)在2019年6月向参议院发表讲话，并解释道：
“没有哪个国家能在未来20年免受气候变化的影响，但一些国家将能够比其他国家更有效地应对、适应或应对。[…]。局部问题可能会溢出，造成全球后果，例如通过增加的人口流离失所、自然资源争端、商品价格波动“[…]。“气候变化的影响可能会破坏美国及其伙伴严重依赖的重要国际体系，如贸易路线、食品和能源供应、全球经济以及国外的国内稳定。”
还有其他风险才刚刚开始被计算在内。在一个相互联系的世界里，那些往往是不可预见的，但又是不可避免的。
首先，在加速向可再生能源过渡的过程中，企业供应链的管理以及消费者习惯的颠覆性变化将导致经济冲突。这不仅仅是易受自然气候干扰的地区生产来源不稳定的影响。随着金融业(如养老基金)将资本转向与ESG挂钩的投资，公开市场上的商品价格也需要稳定下来。
例如，可口可乐(Coca Cola)看到其资产负债表受到干旱的威胁，干旱减少了生产汽水所需的水供应，他们认识到这一风险将被放大。由于热带风暴袭击其主要位于东南亚的工厂的增加，耐克在其供应链管理方面也敲响了警钟。2008年，由于洪水，耐克在泰国的四家工厂不得不关闭。
北极永冻土的融化和海冰的消退被称为气候危机的“煤矿里的金丝雀”。这是因为北半球这一地区的渐进性和戏剧性的物理变化更为直接和有害，因为这里的气温上升速度是其他地方的两倍。
冰冻圈-地球系统的冻水部分-是我们预计会看到气候变化影响的第一个地方。北极的海冰遵循季节性循环，最小值在9月份，最大值在2月和3月。冰盖冬季积雪和夏季融化的平衡正在倾斜。接下来发生的是影响生态系统和水资源的冰川长度的变化，以及全球海平面的变化(即海洋水量和海洋盆地的体积)。
2015年的《巴黎协定》要求各国将全球变暖控制在比工业化前水平高2摄氏度的范围内，目的是将气温控制在1.5摄氏度以下，以避免气候变化带来的最具破坏性的后果。
如果我们要充分控制排放以实现这些目标，集体行动是必不可少的。理解我们的自然和经济系统的相互联系和脆弱性应该促使人们产生责任感。了解气候危机，以及我们对气候危机的直接体验，是突破它在国家话语中的缺席和最小化的一种方式。它应该促使我们投票、资助并致力于解决方案。
note_en:
Temperatures rose   more quickly in the last few decades than at any point in the past 2000 years. We have recently lived through the  hottest May in record history and have reached uncharted  highs of carbon dioxide levels in the atmosphere. The consequences of our testing the limits of our natural habitat are playing out today, poised to speed up and multiply.
  Season after season, the societal toll of climate change is precipitated by climate-related natural disasters. The frequency and variability of these events are outstripping our ability to mitigate its source -- greenhouse gas emissions -- and our ability to adapt to the heightened risk.
  Climate change is here, now. Natural disasters around the world   caused $160 billion in economic damages in 2018. Devastating storms in the Gulf of the U.S. or the coast of Japan have led to lives lost and economic destruction of   upward of $650 billion over two years. Global human displacement is seeing new highs in the Asia Pacific region, with 60 million people displaced from 2013 and 2015 alone. This is an area where nearly a billion people are   expected to live by 2030.
  The U.S. Department of Defense   called climate change a “threat multiplier.” There are no countries immune to its effects. No clearer was this exemplified than during the Syrian Civil War in 2011. A series of droughts in the region   led to large scale migration of young men to the country’s cities. The socio-economic stresses already present were exacerbated by the influx. Less rainfall and prolonged drought in East Africa causing further entrenched conflicts over natural resources is also representative of how anthropogenic climate change is fueling the fire of conflict.
 A study by the Pentagon last year   warned of the link between climate change, war, and migration -- confirming that global temperature rise has and will fuel conflict and refugees. A few months later, Rod Schoonover, a member of the White House National Security Council, spoke to the Senate in June 2019, and explained:
 “No country will be immune to the effect of climate change over the next 20 years, but some will be able to cope, adapt, or respond more effectively than others. […] Local problems could spillover with global consequences, such as through increased human displacement, natural resource disputes, commodity price volatility” […] “Climate change effects could undermine important international systems on which the U.S. and its partners are critically dependent, such as trade routes, food and energy supplies, the global economy, and domestic stability abroad.”
  There are also other risks that have only begun to be accounted for. Those that are often unforeseen but inescapable in an interconnected world.
 For one, the management of corporate supply chains in an accelerating transition to renewable energy and disruptive changes to consumer habits will lead to economic collisions. It is not only the impact of destabilizing production sources in geographies vulnerable to physical climate disruptions. The prices of goods will also need to be stabilized in the open market as the financial industry, like pension funds, shift capital to ESG-pegged investments.
 As an example, Coca Cola saw its balance sheets   endangered by droughts that reduced water availability needed to produce its soda, a risk they recognize will magnify. Nike has also sounded the alarm in its supply chain management due to the increase of tropical storms hitting its factories that mostly reside in Southeast Asia. Four Nike factories in Thailand had to shut down in 2008 due to floods.
  The thawing of permafrost and retreating sea ice in the Arctic has been said to be the “canary in the coal mine” of the climate crisis. That’s because the gradual and dramatic physical changes to this area of the Northern Hemisphere are more immediate and detrimental as temperature increase here  happens twice as fast as everywhere else.
 The cryosphere  -- the frozen water part of the Earth system -- is the first place we expect to see impacts of climate change. Sea ice in the Arctic follows a seasonal cycle with minimums in September and maximums in February and March. Ice sheets’ equilibrium of snow during the winter and melt during the summer is tipping. What happens next is a change in glacier length affecting ecosystems and water resources and a change in global sea level (i.e., the volume of ocean water and volume of the ocean basins).
  The Paris Agreement of 2015 asked that countries limit warming to 2 degrees Celsius above preindustrial levels, with the aim of keeping the temperature below 1.5 degrees Celsius to avoid the most devastating consequences of climate change.
 Collective action is indispensable if we are to adequately rein in emissions to meet those targets. Understanding the interconnectedness and fragility of our natural and economic systems should prompt a sense of responsibility. Understanding the climate crisis, and our direct experience with it is a way to break through its absence and minimization in the national discourse. It should prompt us to vote, fund, and work on the solutions.
